hero.title: Love Story
hero.reason: In honor of National Love Day, this classic romance film tells a poignant tale of two young lovers from different worlds, making it a timeless choice for today.
hero.blurb: This iconic film, starring Ryan O'Neal and Ali MacGraw, is a beautifully told story of love and loss that continues to captivate audiences with its emotional depth and memorable performances. It's a must-watch for anyone who believes in the power of true love.

released.title: The Da Vinci Code
released.reason: Released today in 2006, this mystery thriller, directed by Ron Howard and based on Dan Brown's bestselling novel, still intrigues audiences with its complex web of secrets and conspiracies.
released.blurb: This film, starring Tom Hanks as symbologist Robert Langdon, is a gripping adventure that takes viewers on a thrilling journey through ancient mysteries and hidden symbols. With its clever plot twists and stunning visuals, it's a cinematic experience that will keep you on the edge of your seat.

birthday.title: Peggy Sue Got Married
birthday.reason: Happy birthday to Kathleen Turner, born May 16, 1954, who shines in this nostalgic comedy-drama as a high school graduate who travels back in time to relive her past.
birthday.blurb: This charming film, directed by Francis Ford Coppola, is a delightful blend of humor, romance, and self-discovery, with Turner delivering a standout performance as the titular character. It's a heartwarming and entertaining movie that will leave you smiling.

awful.title: Ballistic: Ecks vs. Sever
awful.reason: This mind-bogglingly bad action film, with its convoluted plot and cringe-worthy dialogue, is a mesmerizing trainwreck that will leave you questioning the life choices of its cast and crew.
awful.blurb: With its laughable performances, atrocious editing, and nonsensical storyline, this movie is a masterclass in how to make a cinematic disaster. It's a fascinatingly awful film that will have you wondering how it ever got made.
